title: (No AI) Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2157758001.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Dave Eggers attends the "The Turning Point: To Be Destroyed" premiere. | Image: John Lamparski/Getty Images for Tribeca Festival

Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless [novels](https://www.theverge.com/2013/8/15/4623732/dave-eggers-the-circle-initial-synopsis), screenplays, pieces of journalism, started [McSweeney's](https://www.theverge.com/art-club/660587/mcsweeneys-quarterly-78-vietnamese-diaspora-cigar-box), and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the _[Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/1c833e7b-3cba-4b87-8b45-33d6c2bd468e?syn-25a6b1a6=1)_, Eggers told the staff:

> "The effect of ChatGPT on educators' lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you've made every teache …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/967630/dave-eggers-openai-chatgpt-silencing-an-entire-generation)

So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the Financial Times, Eggers told the staff:
"The effect of ChatGPT on educators' lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you've made every teache …

Read the full story at The Verge.

So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer […]

title: (No AI) The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
author: David Pierce
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Installer-136.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Hi, friends! Welcome to _Installer_ No. 136, your guide to the best and _Verge_\-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the [_Installer_ homepage](https://www.theverge.com/installer-newsletter).)

This week, I've been recording the next season of [**_Version History_**](https://pod.link/1840983742) (this season's finale is out on Sunday!), reading about [**data center heists**](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/12/magazine/data-center-heist.html) and [**_Backyard Baseball_**](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/13/style/backyard-baseball-video-game-teacher.html?partner=slack&smid=sl-share&__readwiseLocation=) and [**the creator of** **_Calvin and Hobbes_**](https://therepublicofletters.substack.com/p/calvin-and-hobbes-and-the-price-of?__readwiseLocation=), canceling my October plans to see [**_Digger_**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qORTe1wW3Wg) 30 or 40 times, taking on the [new Knockout Tour routes](https://www.nintendo.com/au/news-and-articles/mario-kart-world-update-adds-two-new-routes-to-knockout-tour-and-more/?srsltid=AfmBOorzvCLewxvYX4v9Vzght0LGU7akZC1DpTgmarrRX79wmvGkAsAy) in [**_Mario Kart World_**](https://www.nintendo.com/us/store/products/mario-kart-world-switch-2/?srsltid=AfmBOooYlHpV6SL5bZlSuyHL3kTL8SvgvUOrm11bmSclYqc86oxYL2r5), learning more than I ever intended about Staten Island thanks to [**_Revisionist His …_**](https://www.pushkin.fm/podcasts/revisionist-history/the-staten-island-problem-part-1-the-mayor-vs-the-borough-president)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/967544/best-apps-gadgets-reading-installer)

title: (No AI) A scorecard for the AI age
content: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.
contentSnippet: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

title: (No AI) How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage

content: Now that Google has changed how its usage quotas are tallied, you might not get as many AI responses as you did before.
